summary: Improvement cycle following external review #1 (berendt). Two changes addressed: (1) Makefile e2e-chaos preflight split into a kubectl-reachability probe followed by the chaos-mesh-namespace probe so the two failure modes carry distinct, actionable messages (CC-0097 review #1, comment I-002); (2) external review JSON marked ADDRESSED with a resolution_summary, plus two review-pattern files updated/added to capture the lessons (collapsed failure modes; submodule-pointer cross-file consistency). Architecture submodule pointer was intentionally left unchanged — the upstream architecture-doc update is recorded via the existing PR-link commit (bfc04619).

tests_checklist.item: New kubectl-version preflight failure path has unit-test coverage
Existing tests still pass after Makefile change

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (recipe-level @-prefixed kubectl preflight, sibling style)
Error messages are actionable and target stderr (REQ-007 contract)
Recipe header comment captures rationale and review reference

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (one extra preflight line)
Scope kept to external-review feedback only

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors detected early (kubectl reachability before namespace probe)
Distinct failure modes produce distinct messages

defensive_checklist.item: kubectl version timeout (--request-timeout=2s) prevents hangs against unreachable clusters
Both preflights swallow stdout/stderr and emit a single curated message on stderr

issues_found.description: The new `kubectl version --request-timeout=2s` preflight in Makefile:343 breaks the existing unit test `tests/unit/Makefile/test_e2e_chaos_preflight.sh`. The kubectl stub in that test was deliberately tightened to ONLY accept `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` and to exit 64 with a `[kubectl-stub] unexpected invocation` line on stderr for every other verb — its stated intent (in the inline comment block at lines 53-62) is exactly to surface 'a future change that adds a kubectl call expecting fail-fast behaviour ... here instead of silently passing under a blanket exit 0'. The new `kubectl version` call now triggers that defensive exit, so both test cases regress: test_fast_fail_when_namespace_absent loses 2 of 4 assertions (the stderr no longer contains 'WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra' nor 'chaos-mesh is not installed') and test_happy_path_when_namespace_present fails entirely (make exits 2 instead of 0; chainsaw is never invoked). Verified by running the test against the new Makefile: 4 assertions fail, 5 pass.
issues_found.fix: Update the kubectl stub in tests/unit/Makefile/test_e2e_chaos_preflight.sh to also handle `kubectl version` (return exit 0 when the recipe's reachability probe is expected to succeed). The cleanest shape, consistent with the existing stub's per-verb dispatch, is to add a second branch before the catch-all: `if [ "$1" = "version" ]; then exit 0; fi`. Both existing test cases will then pass again, since the test scenarios assume a reachable cluster.
issues_found.evidence:
Run output:
  Test: e2e-chaos fast-fails when chaos-mesh namespace is absent ...
    PASS: make e2e-chaos exits non-zero when chaos-mesh namespace absent
    FAIL: remediation message on stderr names WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra
      expected to contain: WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra
      actual: kubectl is not configured or no cluster is reachable
    FAIL: remediation message on stderr states chaos-mesh is not installed
      expected to contain: chaos-mesh is not installed
      actual: kubectl is not configured or no cluster is reachable
  ...
  Test: e2e-chaos invokes chainsaw when chaos-mesh namespace present ...
    FAIL: make e2e-chaos exits 0 when chaos-mesh namespace present
      expected: 0
      actual:   2
    FAIL: chainsaw should be invoked when preflight succeeds
  Results: 5 passed, 4 failed, 0 skipped

Stub source (tests/unit/Makefile/test_e2e_chaos_preflight.sh:51-62):
  cat >"$dir/kubectl" <<STUB
  #!/bin/bash
  if [ "\$1" = "get" ] && [ "\$2" = "ns" ] && [ "\$3" = "chaos-mesh" ]; then
    exit ${ns_exit}
  fi
  echo "[kubectl-stub] unexpected invocation: \$*" >&2
  exit 64
  STUB

issues_found.description: The whole point of the I-002 fix is to give the cluster-unreachable case a distinct, actionable message — yet there is no test case that exercises the `kubectl version` failure path. After the BLOCKING fix above, all existing assertions exercise the cluster-reachable branch only; nothing pins the new error string ('kubectl is not configured or no cluster is reachable') or proves the preflight short-circuits before invoking `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` and `chainsaw`. A future regression that drops the version probe, or that prints the wrong message, would not be caught.
issues_found.fix: Add a third test case that stubs kubectl to exit non-zero on `version` (e.g., extend write_stubs with a `version_exit` parameter, or write a separate stub) and asserts: (a) make exits non-zero, (b) stderr contains 'kubectl is not configured or no cluster is reachable', (c) stderr does NOT contain 'chaos-mesh is not installed' (proves short-circuit), (d) chainsaw is not invoked, (e) the chaos-mesh namespace probe is not invoked (proves ordering — the existing strict stub already gives this property for free).
issues_found.evidence: Current test file only defines two test functions: test_fast_fail_when_namespace_absent and test_happy_path_when_namespace_present (tests/unit/Makefile/test_e2e_chaos_preflight.sh:166-167). Neither exercises a non-zero exit from `kubectl version`. The Makefile change at Makefile:343 introduces a new failure path with no corresponding test.

suggested_improvements: When the test stub is updated to add a `version` branch, mirror the stub's existing per-verb tightening style (explicit branch + retain the catch-all `[kubectl-stub] unexpected invocation` exit 64) so any further new kubectl call introduced into the recipe in a future PR still surfaces here instead of silently passing.
Consider parameterizing write_stubs with a third argument `version_exit` (default 0) so the new cluster-unreachable case reuses the same helper instead of duplicating it.

The existing review pattern `verify-cross-file-consistency-after-renames-within-a-pr.md` was extended to cover the I-001 submodule case, but the title and Detection-Hint still talk about renames. Consider broadening the title to 'verify cross-file consistency across the PR scope' or splitting the submodule-pointer concern into its own pattern — the current pattern's hint won't match the next time a submodule pointer is missed.

The Makefile recipe header comment cites 'CC-0097 review #1, comment 2'. Once the external review is merged/closed and the comment numbering is no longer addressable, this reference will rot. Consider citing the commit SHA or a stable identifier instead.
next_steps: BLOCKER: Update tests/unit/Makefile/test_e2e_chaos_preflight.sh kubectl stub so `kubectl version` returns 0 in the existing two test cases, restoring all 4 currently-failing assertions.

MAJOR: Add a third test case that exercises the `kubectl version` non-zero exit path and asserts the new 'kubectl is not configured or no cluster is reachable' message and short-circuit ordering (no namespace probe, no chainsaw invocation).
Re-run `bash tests/unit/Makefile/test_e2e_chaos_preflight.sh` and confirm all assertions pass before re-requesting review.
